objective: Make enforcement policy configuration durable across companion server restarts with safe operator workflows for state changes, approvals, revocation, suspension, restoration, overrides, inspection, auditing, and corrupt-policy recovery.

pilot.reason: No companion track has a current, valid 'qualified' model capability with sufficient shadow routing evidence. Durable Enforcement Policy slice does not change pilot status. Prior pilot selection requirements remain in effect.
requiredForActivation: Companion track with model role having 'qualified' or 'conditional' qualification record (non-candidate/non-screening status)
At least 3-5 shadow routing comparisons showing consistent recommendation agreement
Qualification record must be current (not expired or stale)
Runtime availability and model readiness verifiable

results: 143/143 enforcement policy store tests pass (loading, validation, persistence, state transitions, approval/revocation, overrides, audit, regression, async enforcement gate, audit degradation, audit after-state)
18/18 crew track run record tests pass (backward compatible)
56/56 smoke tests pass (including new enforcement endpoints)
Enforcement remains disabled for all tracks by default

passed: Policy document schema with additionalProperties: false, strict validation
Audit event schema with 10 event types, before/after, revision tracking
Append-only JSONL audit module with schema validation
Synchronous eager init from disk at startup
Async mutations serialized through queue (no concurrent writes)
Atomic persistence: validate → build candidate → validate schema → write temp file → rename → update in-memory → audit
Never truncate target before valid replacement exists
Increment revision exactly once per committed mutation
Full state transition graph strictly enforced: disabled↔shadow→eligible↔enforced, eligible↔suspended, enforced→{eligible,shadow}, suspended→{shadow,eligible,disabled}
Compound mutation operations for approval (eligible + approved) and revocation (eligible→shadow, enforced→suspended)
Override CRUD with composite key identity (trackId + role + modelId), duplicate rejection
Override clear by overrideId or composite key
Corrupt-file fallback with lock preserves existing data, writes audit event
Pure in-memory mode when dataDir not provided (test isolation)
Backward-compatible wrapper (companion/core/enforcement-policy.js) with sync legacy seeding via syncApi
Additive endpoints: GET /enforcement/policy, POST /enforcement/revoke, POST /enforcement/override/clear
Existing endpoints updated with reason/updatedBy parameters
Enforcement policy health in root status (distinguishes healthy from degraded)
No absolute filesystem paths exposed in API responses
Async enforcement gate checks runtime availability, model readiness, and shadow evidence sufficiency (RUNTIME_UNAVAILABLE, MODEL_NOT_READY, INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE)
Enforcement gate checks include approval, qualified capability, score threshold, and active override (all synchronous pre-checks)
Audit degradation surfaced in store health (auditHealthy flag) and mutation results (POLICY_AUDIT_WRITE_FAILED warning)
defaultState locked to const: 'shadow' in policy schema — unsafe non-shadow defaults rejected at validation
expiresAt removed from schema, store, wrapper, and server — no unused dead field
Revocation audit after-state contains actual committed state (not null)
Override creation audit contains generated overrideId (not null)
All prior acceptance criteria remain passing
